sections.paragraph:
τιμωρητής, οῦ, ὁ (τιμωρέω; 2 Macc 4:16; Philo, Rer. Div. Her. 109; PCairMasp 5, 16) avenger, punisher, of the punishing shepherd ὁ ποιμὴν ὁ τιμωρητής Hs 7:1 Joly. ὁ ἄγγελος ὁ τιμ. 7:1, 6 (cp. ὁ τιμωρὸς δαίμων Herm. Wr. 1, 23; Lydus, Mens. 90, 24).—DELG s.v. τιμωρό.

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

PCairMasp PCairMasp = JMaspero, Papyrus grecs d’époque byzantine—List 4

Hs Hs = Similitudes

Joly Joly = Hermas, Le Pasteur; s. under H—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

Lydus Lydus = Joannes Laurentius Lydus, VI a.d.—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1006
